heading: Departure of vessel forbidden for false statements
label: 966 Departure of vessel forbidden for false statements
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 18, U.S.C., 1940 ed., §§ 35, 36 (, title V, §§ 5, 6, ; , ).June 15, 1917, ch. 3040 Stat. 222Mar. 28, 1940, ch. 72, § 554 Stat. 79Section consolidates said sections of title 18, U.S.C., 1940 ed.Mandatory punishment provision was rephrased in the alternative.The phrase “by the head of the department or agency charged with the administration of laws relating to clearance of vessels,” was substituted for “by the Secretary of Commerce” in view of Executive Order No. 9083 (F.R. 1609) transferring functions to the Commissioner of Customs.The conspiracy provision of said section 36 was omitted as covered by . See reviser’s note under that section.section 371 of this titleMinor changes of phraseology were made.

children.label: a
children.text: Whenever it appears that the vessel is not entitled to clearance or whenever there is reasonable cause to believe that the additional statements under oath required in are false, the collector of customs for the district in which the vessel is located may, subject to review by the head of the department or agency charged with the administration of laws relating to clearance of vessels, refuse clearance to any vessel, domestic or foreign, and by formal notice served upon the owners, master, or person or persons in command or charge of any domestic vessel for which clearance is not required by law, forbid the departure of the vessel from the port or from the United States. It shall thereupon be unlawful for the vessel to depart.section 965 of this title

children.label: b
children.text: Whoever, in violation of this section, takes or attempts to take, or authorizes the taking of any such vessel, out of port or from the United States, shall be fined under this title or imprisoned not more than ten years, or both.In addition, such vessel, her tackle, apparel, furniture, equipment, and her cargo shall be forfeited to the United States.
